label: Douglas Adams
wiki_text: Douglas Adams was an English author and humorist best known for The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy. He also wrote for Doctor Who and used science, comedy, and technology as recurring themes.

label: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
description: comedy science fiction franchise created by Douglas Adams
enwiki_title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
wiki_text: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy began as a radio comedy and expanded into novels, television, stage shows, comics, and a film. The story follows Arthur Dent after Earth is destroyed.

label: human
description: common name of Homo sapiens, unique extant species of the genus Homo
wiki_text: A human is a member of Homo sapiens. Wikidata uses human as an instance type for people such as writers and scientists.

label: writer
description: person who uses written words to communicate ideas
wiki_text: A writer is a person who creates written work, including novels, essays, scripts, and reference material.

label: Albert Einstein
wiki_text: Albert Einstein developed the theory of relativity and is one of the most influential physicists. His work shaped modern physics and cosmology.

label: physicist
wiki_text: A physicist studies matter, energy, spacetime, and fundamental interactions. Physicists develop theories and experiments to explain natural phenomena.

label: Nobel Prize in Physics
description: annual award by the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences
wiki_text: The Nobel Prize in Physics is awarded for outstanding contributions in physics. Albert Einstein received the 1921 prize for his explanation of the photoelectric effect.
